releases.headline: API consolidation for upcoming sync client
releases.summary: Access control, auth-failure responses, and audit-log shape are normalized so a non-browser sync client can talk to the same endpoints as the web UI without each route reinventing its own JSON contract.

items.title: Shared `require-access` middleware
items.body: Bundles and stashes now share a single `require-access.js` middleware for access checks, so password, email gating, and domain gating evaluate identically across both resource types.

items.title: Structured JSON audit log for file mutations
items.body: File create, rename, and delete events emit a structured JSON audit record (actor, resource, action, outcome) instead of a free-form log line, so downstream tooling can consume the stream without regex parsing.

items.title: JSON content negotiation on bundle view
items.body: Requesting a bundle view with `Accept: application/json` returns the bundle metadata + file list as JSON instead of the HTML template, enabling programmatic clients without a separate API surface.

items.title: `requireAuth` content-negotiates failure response
items.body: Authentication failures now return `401` with a JSON body for API clients (Accept: application/json) and a browser redirect for HTML clients, instead of always redirecting.

releases.headline: Email and domain gating for Customer Stash pages
releases.summary: Customer Stash pages can now be restricted to a recipient allow list — either specific email addresses or entire domains such as `@acme.com` — using the same one-time-code verification flow already used for bundles.

items.title: Stash access restricted by email or domain
items.body: A stash can be restricted to a list of specific email addresses or whole domains (`@acme.com`), so only recipients matching the allow list can open the page after verification.

items.title: One-time verification code on stashes
items.body: The stash verification endpoint reuses the bundle-side anti-enumeration shape: the response is identical whether the supplied email matches the allow list or not, so attackers can't probe the recipient list.

items.title: Dual protection on stashes
items.body: A stash can require both email verification and a password, so a recipient must hold the password and prove control of an allow-listed email before the page renders.

items.title: Stash editor field for email/domain allow list
items.body: Admin UI gains a Restrict to emails/domains field on the stash editor, mirroring the bundle-side field so the workflow is identical across the two access-gated surfaces.

releases.headline: Configurable analytics integration
releases.summary: Admins can paste any analytics provider's script tag into the branding panel and have it injected on public pages with the right CSP allowance — no provider-specific integration code required.

items.title: Provider-agnostic analytics field
items.body: Admin > Branding gains a field that accepts any analytics provider's script tag. The tag is injected on public pages only; admin and dashboard surfaces are excluded so authenticated activity never reaches the analytics provider.

items.title: Automatic CSP allowance for analytics domains
items.body: The injected script tag is parsed to extract the analytics origin, which is then added to the Content-Security-Policy `script-src` and `connect-src` allowlists. A manual override is available for providers that load assets from a different host than the script tag.

items.title: Tested with common providers
items.body: Verified with Plausible, Umami, Matomo, Fathom, PostHog, and Google Analytics. Any provider that follows the standard script-tag-on-page integration pattern should work without provider-specific code.

releases.headline: SEO, legal pages, and accessibility improvements

items.title: Open Graph and Twitter Card metadata
items.body: Public pages emit Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags plus a canonical URL, while auth pages emit `noindex` so credentials surfaces don't appear in search results.

items.title: `robots.txt` and dynamic `sitemap.xml`
items.body: A `robots.txt` blocks crawlers from `/admin`, `/dashboard`, and `/vault`, and a dynamically generated `sitemap.xml` lists the public surfaces that should be indexed.

items.title: Configurable legal pages
items.body: Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, and Cookie Policy pages are now configurable from admin, with built-in defaults so a fresh install ships with usable legal copy.

items.title: Skip-to-content link and ARIA labels
items.body: Every page gets a keyboard-accessible skip-to-content link, and interactive controls receive ARIA labels and accessible names so screen-reader navigation matches visual navigation.

items.title: Alt text on all images
items.body: Every shipped `` carries descriptive alt text (or empty alt for decorative images), so non-sighted users get an equivalent reading of every page.

items.title: Semantic HTML with `main` landmark
items.body: Every page wraps its primary content in a `<main>` landmark, so assistive technology can jump straight to the content region without traversing chrome.

releases.headline: Stored-XSS fix, email validation, and password minimum

items.title: Stored XSS in bundle names
items.body: Bundle names rendered with triple-brace (unescaped) template syntax now render with double-brace escaping, so HTML in a bundle name can no longer execute in another viewer's browser.

items.title: Server-side password minimum on bundles and stashes
items.body: Bundle and stash passwords are rejected by the backend if shorter than 4 characters, so a manipulated frontend can no longer set a 1- or 2-character password.

items.title: Uploader email validated via shared helper
items.body: Bundle init now runs the uploader email through the shared `validateEmail()` instead of the previous inline check, matching the rule applied everywhere else email is captured.

releases.headline: Upload handler consolidation removes ~320 duplicated lines
releases.summary: The upload pipeline is collapsed into a single shared handler so drop and stash routes no longer carry parallel copies of the same chunking, quota, and finalize logic.

items.title: Shared upload handler module
items.body: `upload.handler.js` now owns `handleFileUpload`, `handleChunkUpload`, and `handleFinalize`. `drop.js` shrinks from 397 to 107 lines and the stash upload section drops from 340 to 70 lines, eliminating roughly 320 lines of duplication.

items.title: Consolidated quota checking
items.body: Storage, per-user, and per-IP quota gates are evaluated in a single call instead of three separate code paths, so a quota refusal returns the same response shape regardless of which limit tripped.

items.title: Stash config resolution extracted
items.body: The stash config lookup that was inlined in three places is now a single helper, so changes to how stash defaults are resolved propagate uniformly.

items.title: Shared frontend helpers
items.body: `showRenameError`, `esc`, and `formatSize` move into a shared `helpers.js`, so the inline copies in individual page scripts are gone.

items.title: Email validation uses shared `validateEmail()`
items.body: Inline regex email checks across upload and access-gate paths now call the shared `validateEmail()` helper, matching the rule applied at user signup.

releases.headline: CSRF enforcement and Zip Slip path-traversal fixes

items.title: CSRF enforcement on non-JSON cookie-authenticated POSTs
items.body: Non-JSON POST requests to cookie-authenticated routes now return 403 unless they carry a valid CSRF token, closing a gap where form-encoded requests could bypass the token check.

items.title: Zip Slip path-traversal mitigation
items.body: ZIP entry names have `..` segments stripped during extraction, and `sanitizeFilename()` strips path traversal from every upload, so neither archive contents nor direct uploads can write outside their intended directory.

releases.headline: Email-gated bundle access without passwords
releases.summary: Bundles can now be restricted to a specific allow list of recipient emails, with access proven by a one-time code instead of (or in addition to) a password. The verification path is anti-enumeration: the response is identical whether the supplied email is on the allow list or not.

items.title: Email-gated bundles
items.body: Uploaders can restrict bundle access to a specific list of recipient emails. Recipients prove ownership of the email by entering a one-time code sent to that address.

items.title: Dual protection mode
items.body: A bundle can require both email verification and a password, so the recipient must hold the password and demonstrate control of an allow-listed email address before download.

items.title: Anti-enumeration verification response
items.body: The verification endpoint returns an identical response whether the supplied email is on the allow list or not, preventing attackers from probing membership of the recipient list.

items.title: One-time access codes
items.body: Codes are 6 digits, expire in 10 minutes, allow 5 attempts, and are stored as SHA3 hashes — only the hash is compared during verification.

items.title: `bundle_access_codes` and `bundle_access_log` tables
items.body: Two new tables track issued codes and successful verifications. All fields are vault-sealed; only identifiers, counters, and timestamps remain raw.

items.title: Restrict to emails option in upload form
items.body: The upload form gains a Restrict to emails field so an uploader can attach the allow list at bundle-creation time without a separate admin step.

items.title: Hourly cleanup of expired access codes
items.body: A background sweep deletes expired entries from `bundle_access_codes` once per hour so the table doesn't accumulate stale rows.

releases.headline: Dashboard overhaul, bundle naming, inline rename, shared sanitization
releases.summary: Dashboard is redesigned around row-based lists with bundle and file drill-down. Bundles can be named at upload time and renamed inline (along with files and vault batches), all validation now happens on the backend, and upload handling gains magic-byte checks, per-IP storage quota, and consistent filename sanitization.

items.title: Row-based dashboard with drill-down
items.body: Dashboard is redesigned from card grids to row-based lists, with bundle and file drill-down for faster navigation in stashes with many entries.

items.title: Bundle naming at upload + inline rename
items.body: Bundles can be named during upload, and bundles, files, and vault batches can be renamed inline. All rename validation runs on the backend; the frontend sends raw input and displays the server response.

items.title: Shared filename sanitization helper
items.body: New `sanitize-filename.js` exports `sanitizeRename()`, `sanitizeFilename()`, and `safeContentDisposition()` so every upload, rename, and download path uses the same sanitization rules.

items.title: Per-IP storage quota
items.body: Upload path now tracks bytes per source IP and refuses writes that would exceed the configured per-IP ceiling, in addition to the existing per-user and global quota gates.

items.title: Magic-byte upload validation
items.body: Uploads are validated against expected magic bytes for their declared MIME type, rejecting renamed payloads before they reach storage.

items.title: Allow All extension toggle
items.body: Admins can remove the file extension whitelist with an Allow All button instead of editing the list to match an open policy.

items.title: Shop Merch link in navbar and footer
items.body: When maintainer support is enabled, the navbar and footer surface a Shop Merch link pointing at the configured merch URL.

items.title: Expanded field-level encryption coverage
items.body: Field-level encryption now covers additional columns across every table, narrowing the set of raw values to identifiers, counters, status enums, timestamps, and foreign keys.

items.title: Stale bundle cleanup deletes orphan files
items.body: The stale-bundle sweep now also removes the underlying file rows and on-disk payloads when their parent bundle is reaped, instead of leaving orphaned blobs behind.
